purpose: Make MiniMax vLLM/XPU graph benchmarks distinguish cold AOT compile artifacts from steady-state warm AOT decode.
changes: Added RUN_TIMEOUT/RUN_TIMEOUT_KILL_AFTER support to the base vLLM MiniMax benchmark wrapper so CCL or graph-load stalls are bounded.
Added a warm-AOT wrapper that reuses one VLLM_CACHE_ROOT for an optional warmup process and a separate measured process.

Default warm-AOT recipe remains the current quality-preserving graph path: TP4, float16 activations, llm-scaler INT4 MoE, XPU graph enabled, graph partition, compile_sizes=[1], cudagraph_mode=PIECEWISE.

first_smoke.name: reused_cache_wrapper_attempt
first_smoke.notes: The wrapper skipped warmup because an AOT model file was present, then the measured process stopped advancing after XCCL rank initialization. No JSON was produced, so this is not a benchmark result. The process was terminated and RUN_TIMEOUT was added before further experiments.

next_use: Use a fresh isolated VLLM_CACHE_ROOT plus FORCE_WARMUP=1 for source-level variants, then compare only the measured warm-AOT process against the 69.064767 tok/s reference.
